data.title: Mitsubishi UFJ: Gold futures are poised for a weekly gain.
data.description: Gold futures are poised for a weekly gain as optimism surrounding potential talks between the US and Iran and a ceasefire agreement between Israel and Lebanon is weighing on oil prices and boosting overall market sentiment. Mitsubishi UFJ stated, "Lower oil prices have eased inflation concerns." However, while gold has recovered some of its earlier losses, it remains about 9% lower than at the start of the conflict, and continued risks to energy supplies and the presence of damaged infrastructure could keep inflation uncertainty high. (Jinshi)

data.title: Bitcoin Miners Shift Focus to AI Amid Declining Mining Revenue
data.description:
Bitcoin miners are increasingly pivoting towards artificial intelligence (AI) ventures, according to Charles Edwards, founder of Capriole. According to Foresight News, Edwards noted that all major publicly listed Bitcoin mining companies have announced significant shifts to AI operations. He predicts that within two to three years, the proportion of revenue from Bitcoin mining will drop from the current 90% to just 30%. Edwards expressed concern over this trend, especially with the looming threat of quantum computing, as the security foundation of the Bitcoin network, based on computational power, is rapidly diminishing. Many miners are reportedly not planning to upgrade or purchase new Bitcoin mining equipment.

In response, Adam Back, CEO of Blockstream, suggested that this shift could be beneficial for miners. He explained that if the overall network's computational power decreases, mining profit margins could actually increase. This situation presents an arbitrage opportunity, eventually leading to an equilibrium where mining profit margins align with those of AI workloads. Higher profit margins could create a positive feedback loop, where miners sell less Bitcoin to cover electricity costs, and as Bitcoin prices rise, profit margins would further improve.

data.title: Hong Kong Halts Basketball Betting Project Amid Concerns Over Illegal Gambling
data.description:
The Hong Kong government has decided to halt the advancement of a basketball betting project, according to a report by Hong Kong Radio on April 17. According to BlockBeats, Secretary for Home and Youth Affairs, Mak Mei-juan, stated that the rapid development of the 'prediction market' in recent years has raised concerns. Monthly trading volumes have surged from less than $100 million two years ago to over $13 billion last year, with nearly 40% of transactions related to sports. Authorities worry that introducing a new betting project now could increase public interest and participation in illegal gambling within the 'prediction market.'

Mak emphasized that existing regulated betting projects have established mature monitoring and management systems, which help control risks. However, the uncertainty surrounding the 'prediction market' necessitates further research to understand its impact and prevalence in Hong Kong. The government maintains close contact with the Hong Kong Jockey Club, which has a stringent monitoring system for illegal gambling and employs experts to combat such activities. The government will collaborate with the Jockey Club to develop measures specifically targeting illegal gambling.

Mak reiterated the government's longstanding policy of discouraging gambling. She noted that betting on sports events within the 'prediction market' constitutes illegal gambling. The authorities plan to enhance public education and awareness campaigns to highlight the dangers of gambling and discourage participation in illegal activities.

data.title: Middle East Sees Increased Dealmaking Amid Regional Disruptions
data.description: Abu Dhabi's Axight is contributing to a surge in dealmaking activities across the Middle East, despite ongoing disruptions caused by the Iran war. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the resilience of the region's business environment in the face of geopolitical tensions. The Middle East has witnessed a series of significant deals, underscoring the area's strategic importance and economic potential. Companies are navigating challenges posed by regional conflicts, yet continue to pursue growth and expansion opportunities. This trend reflects a broader determination to maintain economic momentum and capitalize on emerging markets within the region.

data.title: Binance Unveils Capital Connect Upgrade and Wallet Enhancements
data.description:
Binance announced on X the latest updates to its platform, focusing on enhancing user experience and connectivity. The key highlight is the upgrade to Capital Connect, which now features a strategy marketplace. This new addition aims to facilitate direct connections between professional trading teams and institutional investors, making collaboration more accessible. This development is expected to streamline interactions and foster a more integrated trading environment.

In addition to the Capital Connect upgrade, Binance has introduced significant enhancements to its wallet interface. The updated wallet now includes sliding cards on the market page, providing users with a more intuitive and efficient way to navigate through market data. Furthermore, a customizable quick access feature has been added, allowing users to tailor their experience according to their preferences. These improvements are designed to make operations smoother and more user-friendly, reflecting Binance's commitment to continuous innovation and user satisfaction.

data.title: Japan's Largest Union Reports Wage Increase Over 5%
data.description:
Japan's largest labor union, the Japanese Trade Union Confederation (Rengo), announced that the average wage increase for employees of its member companies has exceeded 5% in this year's wage negotiations. According to Jin10, this trend persists even when considering the results from smaller enterprises. This development is expected to facilitate the Bank of Japan's short-term interest rate hike goals.

The latest statistics released on Friday indicate that unions affiliated with Rengo have achieved an average wage growth of 5.08%. Although this figure is slightly lower than the initially reported 5.26%, it remains above the organization's 5% target for the third consecutive year. The recent data shows that 2,156 companies with 300 or fewer employees have reached wage increase agreements, a significant rise from the 552 companies reported in March's initial report. Smaller companies typically report wage agreements later than larger firms, which has contributed to a decrease in the overall average in recent years.

data.title: UBS Report: Fed Expected to Cut Rates by 50 Basis Points Later This Year
data.description: UBS released a report on April 17, indicating that the Federal Reserve remains on a path toward further easing. According to Jin10, Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell recently downplayed the need to tighten monetary policy due to rising energy prices, noting that policymakers typically 'ignore' supply shocks like oil price surges, especially when inflation expectations remain well-controlled. Although the Fed is still seeking further evidence of a sustained decline in core inflation before implementing additional easing measures, UBS still expects a 50 basis point rate cut later this year. Given that U.S. Treasury yields are significantly higher than pre-conflict levels, UBS believes there is ample room for them to decrease. The year-end targets for the 2-year and 10-year Treasury yields are set at 3.25% and 3.75%, respectively.

data.title: UBS Report: ECB Likely to Maintain Rates Until Year-End Despite Hawkish Tone
data.description: UBS has released a report indicating that despite the European Central Bank's (ECB) hawkish stance, it is expected to keep interest rates unchanged until the end of the year. According to Jin10, ECB President Christine Lagarde stated this week that rising energy costs have shifted the eurozone away from the central bank's baseline scenario outlook, prompting the ECB to evaluate its options. Given the ECB's inflation mandate and its forecast that the impact of the Iran conflict on inflation will outweigh its effect on growth, current market pricing suggests the ECB will raise rates twice by year-end. However, the current economic environment differs significantly from the onset of the Russia-Ukraine conflict in 2022. The ECB's policy has only recently returned to a neutral setting, and the labor market has remained weak. Considering the risks to growth prospects posed by the conflict and the tightening financial conditions already evident in the bond market, UBS believes the ECB is unlikely to rush into rate hikes and will likely maintain rates until the end of the year.

data.title: Analysts: Limited upside for European stocks this year
data.description: According to the average forecast of 17 analysts, the Stoxx Europe 600 index will be about 1% higher than Wednesday's closing price, reaching 623 points by the end of 2026. The index has largely recovered from the losses suffered due to the Iran war, but strategists say this rebound seems unsustainable as the full impact of rising energy costs has not yet materialized. Deutsche Bank, DekaBank, UBS, and UniCredit all moderately lowered their index targets in their latest surveys, with HSBC being the most bullish, forecasting 670 points, implying an 8% increase. TFS Futures and Bank of America remain the most bearish strategists, predicting a potential 9% decline in the index. (Jinshi)

data.title: OKX Agent Trade Kit launches "Sentiment Radar" to enhance its ability to capture market information and sentiment.
data.description: According to official sources, OKX Agent Trade Kit has launched the "Sentiment Radar" feature, integrating three core capabilities: news aggregation, sentiment analysis, and anomaly alerts. It aggregates information from multiple mainstream industry media outlets, including "Odaily," and over 30,000 core encrypted KOLs globally. Through quantitative sentiment analysis and anomaly signal identification, it captures real-time changes in market sentiment and potential trends. Reportedly, OKX Agent Trade Kit has also simultaneously launched the "Market Screening and Position Analysis Suite." By combining market fund flows, market news, and sentiment, users can identify potential trading opportunities from multi-dimensional data, achieving a comprehensive judgment of market trends and fund flows. The two functions can be cross-validated.

data.title: Iran says it is ready to retaliate forcefully against enemy aggression.
data.description: The Iranian Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps (IRGC) issued a statement on the 17th, saying that the IRGC and the Iranian army are prepared to respond forcefully to any aggression or criminal acts by the enemy. The statement said that the enemy's attempts to invade Iran through land and sea operations and seize Iranian islands in the Persian Gulf have been thwarted. If the enemy dares to make any rash moves in its aggression against Iran, it will undoubtedly face a fatal counterattack from Iran, gaining nothing but self-humiliation and a humiliating strategic defeat. (Xinhua)

data.title: UBS: Still expects the Fed to cut rates by 50 basis points later this year.
data.description: A UBS research report points out that the Federal Reserve remains on track for further easing. Fed Chairman Powell recently downplayed the need to tighten monetary policy due to rising energy prices, noting that policymakers typically "ignore" supply shocks such as soaring oil prices, especially when inflation expectations remain firmly under control. While the Fed is still seeking further evidence of a sustained decline in core inflation before implementing further easing, we still expect a 50 basis point rate cut later this year. Given that US Treasury yields are significantly higher than pre-conflict levels, we believe there is ample downside potential, and our year-end targets for 2-year and 10-year Treasury yields are 3.25% and 3.75%, respectively. (Jinshi)

data.title: Quarterly Transactions Surpass 200 Million for the First Time in Q1 2026
data.description:
Quarterly transactions reached a significant milestone in the first quarter of 2026, surpassing 200.4 million for the first time. According to CoinDesk, this figure represents more than double the transaction lows recorded in 2023. The surge in transactions highlights a notable increase in activity within the market, reflecting broader trends and shifts in user engagement and adoption.

The increase in transactions can be attributed to various factors, including technological advancements, increased accessibility, and growing interest in digital assets. As the market continues to evolve, the rise in transaction volume underscores the expanding role of digital currencies in the global financial landscape. This development is indicative of a broader acceptance and integration of digital assets into everyday financial activities, suggesting a shift in how transactions are conducted and perceived.

The data from the first quarter of 2026 marks a pivotal moment in the history of digital transactions, setting a new benchmark for future quarters. As the industry continues to grow, stakeholders are closely monitoring these trends to better understand the implications for the market and its participants. The doubling of transactions since 2023 highlights the rapid pace of change and the potential for further growth in the coming years.

data.title: Haun Ventures Sees Growth Amid Mastercard's Acquisition of BVNK
data.description: Haun Ventures has experienced growth despite market trends, driven by Mastercard's acquisition of BVNK. According to PANews, this strategic move has positioned Haun Ventures favorably within the stablecoin sector. The acquisition highlights the increasing interest and investment in stablecoins, which are gaining traction in the financial industry.

data.title: Listed Bitcoin mining companies sold over 32,000 BTC in Q1 2026, exceeding the total for the entire year of 2025.
data.description: Publicly listed Bitcoin mining companies sold a total of over 32,000 BTC in the first quarter of 2026, exceeding the total sell-off for the entire year of 2025 and setting a new quarterly record. According to data from TheMinerMag, the companies involved include MARA, CleanSpark, Riot, Cango, Core Scientific, and Bitdeer. The report also states that the current miner hash rate is approximately $33 per PH/s per day, below the break-even point of around $35 for some mining companies. Furthermore, according to CryptoQuant data, Bitcoin miner reserves have decreased from over 1.86 million BTC since 2023 to approximately 1.8 million BTC. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 75,000 USDT with a 0.01% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Apr 17, 2026, 07:02 AM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 75,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 75,011.539063 USDT, with a narrowed 0.01% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: BNB Price Projections: Potential Gains and Future Targets
data.description: Crypto Patel has highlighted that historical accumulation zones for BNB have often led to significant price increases, with potential targets set at $2,000, $5,000, and $10,000. According to NS3.AI, CoinCodex forecasts BNB reaching $943 by July 2026 and $890.90 by the end of 2026.

data.title: The market capitalization of ASTEROID, a Meme coin on the Ethereum blockchain, briefly surpassed $20 million, and is currently valued at $19.2 million.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the market capitalization of the Ethereum blockchain's Meme coin ASTEROID briefly exceeded $20 million, currently trading at $19.2 million. Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks. Meme coins generally lack real-world applications and rely more on speculative hype; caution is advised when entering the market.

data.title: Bitcoin Price Fluctuates Amid U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Talks
data.description:
Bitcoin's price is currently at $74,700, experiencing a 0.4% decline over the past 24 hours. According to BlockBeats, the news of U.S.-Iran ceasefire negotiations has boosted risk sentiment, leading the S&P 500 index to reach a record high on Thursday. U.S. President Donald Trump expressed optimism about a permanent ceasefire, claiming Iran has agreed to abandon its nuclear ambitions, transfer nuclear materials, and reopen the Strait of Hormuz, although Iran has yet to confirm these concessions.

Meanwhile, the market is closely monitoring structural signals behind Bitcoin's price movements. ZeroStack CEO Daniel Reis-Faria noted that the negative funding rates indicate a market heavily shorting Bitcoin. He suggested that if Bitcoin continues to rise under these conditions, many short positions could face forced liquidation, potentially accelerating price increases. Reis-Faria anticipates that if the short base is cleared, Bitcoin could reach $125,000 within the next 30 to 60 days.

On-chain analyst CryptoVizArt offered another perspective, highlighting Bitcoin's "True Market Mean" (TMM), which shows that the average holding cost for active holders is currently above the market price, resulting in overall unrealized losses. Since 2016, persistent breaches of this mean have often coincided with Bitcoin's most severe downturns, including the 2018-2019 bear market (maximum drop of 57%, lasting 282 days) and the 2022-2023 decline following the Luna and FTX collapses (maximum drop of 56%, lasting 339 days).

Analysts suggest that these two assessments are not mutually exclusive. The negative funding rates could trigger a significant price surge, but the structural pressure from active holders' unrealized losses might eventually lead to selling. The future trajectory may depend on whether the U.S.-Iran ceasefire can be extended beyond its expiration next week.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview Model Poses Cybersecurity Threat to Global Banks
data.description: Financial officials have issued a warning regarding the potential cybersecurity risks posed by Anthropic's Claude Mythos Preview model to the global banking system. According to Odaily, the model is considered a significant threat to the network defenses of banks worldwide. The concerns highlight the growing challenges in securing financial institutions against advanced AI technologies.
